title: Unable to remove container image or container or  start container when host FS is full
body:
### Issue Description

Running a fedora 41 bootable container image on kvm.  Once booted, I pulled some different container images and ran a base fedora image and proceeded to write files in the container which resulted in the host FS becoming full.  Once this happens you're unable to free storage using podman.  I could manually go into the container storage directory for the host and delete everything to get out of this error condition, but that wouldn't be very helpful if I had data of value.  Additionally, once this happens you can't use podman to start up the container to delete files within the container.  What is the intended way to get back to a usable state when this occurs?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a bootable container and run it, eg. `sudo virt-install --name fedora-bootc --cpu host --vcpus 4 --memory 4096 --import --disk disk.qcow2,format=qcow2 --os-variant fedora41`
2. ssh to the running instance and pull some images, eg.  `podman pull fedora` and others
3. start an interactive session with one of the images and proceed to use space


### Describe the results you received

``` bash
...
[root@c47817f9ce84 /]# dd if=/dev/zero of=/small.bin bs=1024 count=10000000
dd: error writing '/small.bin': No space left on device
8190+0 records in
8189+0 records out
8385536 bytes (8.4 MB, 8.0 MiB) copied, 0.0646482 s, 130 MB/s
[root@c47817f9ce84 /]# exit
exit
ERRO[0027] Rolling back transaction to add exit code: cannot rollback - no transaction is active 
ERRO[0027] Waiting for container c47817f9ce84e5a13efcaaeaf332c65a074c26ab13b00be5e537deb7e8569d07: adding container c47817f9ce84e5a13efcaaeaf332c65a074c26ab13b00be5e537deb7e8569d07 exit code 1: database or disk is full 
[root@fedora ~]# df -h
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/vda4       8.6G  8.3G     0 100% /sysroot
...
[root@fedora ~]# podman images
REPOSITORY                         TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9    latest      5c79fba2bcae  7 hours ago   234 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora  latest      b5ea3eaaad47  13 hours ago  164 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8    latest      bb4496e662fb  3 weeks ago   213 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi7    latest      a084eb42a557  8 months ago  219 MB
[root@fedora ~]# podman rmi 5c79fba2bcae
Error: no space left on device
# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                     COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS             PORTS       NAMES
c47817f9ce84  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest  /bin/bash   14 minutes ago  Up About a minute              friendly_mahavira
[root@fedora ~]# podman rm c47817f9ce84
ERRO[0000] Rolling back transaction to add exit code: cannot rollback - no transaction is active 
Error: adding container c47817f9ce84e5a13efcaaeaf332c65a074c26ab13b00be5e537deb7e8569d07 exit code 1: database or disk is full
[root@fedora home]# podman start --interactive --attach c47817f9ce84
ERRO[0000] Rolling back transaction to add exit code: cannot rollback - no transaction is active 
ERRO[0000] Rolling back transaction to save container c47817f9ce84e5a13efcaaeaf332c65a074c26ab13b00be5e537deb7e8569d07 state: cannot rollback - no transaction is active 
Error: unable to start container c47817f9ce84e5a13efcaaeaf332c65a074c26ab13b00be5e537deb7e8569d07: adding container c47817f9ce84e5a13efcaaeaf332c65a074c26ab13b00be5e537deb7e8569d07 exit code 1: database or disk is full


### Describe the results you expected

podman having the ability to recover in this position, especially when the user is running on a host where virtually everything is read-only and the only thing using R/W storage is the container run-time itself.

### podman info output

```yaml
# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.3.2
API Version:  5.3.2
Go Version:   go1.23.4
Built:        Wed Jan 22 00:00:00 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64


# rpm -q podman
podman-5.3.2-1.fc41.x86_64

# cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="41.20250209.0 (Forty One)"
RELEASE_TYPE=stable
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=41
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f41"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora Linux 41.20250209.0 (Forty One)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:41"
DEFAULT_HOSTNAME="fedora"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/f41/system-administrators-guide/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://ask.fedoraproject.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=41
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=41
SUPPORT_END=2025-12-15
OSTREE_VERSION='41.20250209.0'

# podman info
ERRO[0000] Rolling back transaction to add exit code: cannot rollback - no transaction is active 
Error: getting store info: adding container c47817f9ce84e5a13efcaaeaf332c65a074c26ab13b00be5e537deb7e8569d07 exit code 1: database or disk is full
```

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I think it would be good to add a drop down entry for "Podman in a container" for "Yes, bootable container"

comments.body:
@mheon ptal

the disk is full so we cannot apparently write to the database so this makes sense to me.  probably cannot even get a lock on the image to remove it?

Dup of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13967

The consensus there seems to be that the best way forward was to make a moderate-sized (maybe 10mb?) file that we can delete after getting an ENOSPC to clear some space and try and unblock things. Far from perfect but better than nothing.
